skill.name: mermaid-flowchart
skill.description: Reference guide for creating Mermaid flowcharts with nodes, edges, subgraphs, styling, links, shapes, and layout controls.
skill.summary: Create Mermaid flowcharts with accurate syntax for nodes, edges, labels, styling, and interactions.
skill.author: 46ki75

security_audit.summary: The scanner reported many high and medium patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown fences, Mermaid arrows, inline backticks, relative documentation links, and URL examples. A real moderate concern remains: the skill documents interactive Mermaid callbacks, external links, and securityLevel: 'loose', which is safe only for trusted diagrams and controlled renderers. No prompt injection, command execution, filesystem access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Interactive Mermaid Callbacks Require Trusted Rendering
medium_findings.description: The documentation demonstrates JavaScript callbacks, external links, and securityLevel: 'loose'. This is legitimate Mermaid behavior, but it can be unsafe with untrusted diagram text or embedded HTML.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly document callbacks and loose Mermaid security settings. The behavior is legitimate, but unsafe when applied to untrusted content.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby and shell backtick findings match Markdown fences and inline Mermaid syntax. No executable Ruby, shell invocation, or command runner was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled locations are fenced Markdown examples and inline syntax references. There is no Ruby source file or shell execution context.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The '../config/icons.md' strings are relative documentation links, not filesystem reads or writes. No path input or file access API was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are Markdown links to documentation. They do not feed into any filesystem operation.

low_findings.title: Malware Keyword Matches Are Mermaid Examples
low_findings.description: High blocker matches appear on ordinary flowchart terms, links, edges, and subgraph examples. No command and control behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced content is plain Mermaid documentation and diagram syntax. There are no network beacons, payloads, or obfuscation.

low_findings.title: External URL Examples May Trigger Network Activity
low_findings.description: The skill includes example image, link, jsfiddle, GitHub, and CDN URLs. They are documentation examples, but rendered diagrams may load or open external resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation examples, not hidden exfiltration. They still create a real network consideration when rendered or followed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Interactive Mermaid Callbacks Require Trusted Rendering
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation demonstrates JavaScript callbacks, external links, and securityLevel: 'loose'. This is legitimate Mermaid behavior, but it can be unsafe with untrusted diagram text or embedded HTML.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly document callbacks and loose Mermaid security settings. The behavior is legitimate, but unsafe when applied to untrusted content.

content.value_statement: Mermaid flowchart syntax can be hard to remember across shapes, links, labels, and subgraphs. This skill provides focused guidance for building clear diagrams in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains Mermaid flowchart node and edge syntax.
Shows supported graph directions and layout options.
Documents node shapes, subgraphs, labels, and edge styles.
Covers links, callbacks, tooltips, and interaction syntax.
Includes examples for styling, classes, icons, and images.
limitations: It is a reference document, not a diagram renderer.
It does not validate Mermaid syntax automatically.
Interactive callbacks require a renderer that permits them.
Some features depend on the Mermaid version in use.

use_cases.title: Document A Product Flow
use_cases.description: Create a Mermaid flowchart that maps screens, decisions, and user paths for a product specification.

use_cases.title: Explain A System Process
use_cases.description: Turn a technical workflow into a readable flowchart for architecture notes or pull request documentation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Diagrams
use_cases.description: Use consistent shapes, labels, subgraphs, and styling across internal Markdown documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flowchart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple process diagram.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart for this process: [describe steps]. Use clear labels and a top-to-bottom layout.

prompt_templates.title: Decision Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart with decision nodes for this workflow: [describe workflow]. Label each branch clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Grouped System Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for subgraphs and ownership boundaries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart with subgraphs for these teams or systems: [list groups]. Show key handoffs and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Interactive Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for controlled documentation environments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a styled Mermaid flowchart for [topic]. Include classes, link labels, and only safe links approved for this document.

output_examples.input: A login process with success, password reset, and failed authentication paths.
output_examples.output: A top-to-bottom flowchart with start, credential entry, decision branches, reset handling, success, and failure states.

output_examples.input: A deployment workflow across build, test, approval, and release systems.
output_examples.output: A left-to-right diagram with subgraphs for automation, quality checks, human approval, and production release.

output_examples.input: A customer support escalation path with service levels.
output_examples.output: A readable process map with decision nodes, labeled transitions, escalation steps, and final resolution outcomes.

best_practices: Use short node labels and explain details in nearby text.
Keep diagrams readable by limiting chained links and dense styling.
Use strict Mermaid security settings for untrusted content.
anti_patterns: Do not use interactive callbacks with untrusted diagram input.
Do not rely on external images or links without review.
Do not pack too many branches into one flowchart.

faq.question: Does this skill render Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: No. It provides syntax guidance and examples for creating Mermaid flowcharts.

faq.question: Can it help with subgraphs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains subgraph syntax, directions, and links between subgraphs.

faq.question: Does it support custom styling?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents classes, link styles, curves, animations, and related Mermaid styling features.

faq.question: Are interactive links safe?
faq.answer: They are safe only in trusted renderers with reviewed URLs and appropriate Mermaid security settings.

faq.question: Can I use it in Markdown documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. Mermaid flowcharts are commonly embedded in Markdown tools that support Mermaid rendering.

faq.question: Does Mermaid version matter?
faq.answer: Yes. Some shapes, icons, and animation features require newer Mermaid versions.
